questions.question: According to the harmful dysfunction model, a psychological disorder exists when which two conditions are met?

options.text: The behavior violates social norms and is disapproved of by the majority of people
A medical test confirms a brain abnormality and the person cannot work
The behavior is statistically rare and the person feels distressed about it
An internal mechanism fails its natural function and this failure causes harm to the individual

questions.explanation: Jerome Wakefield's harmful dysfunction model requires both an internal dysfunction (a psychological mechanism failing its evolved function) and harm to the individual as judged by cultural standards.

questions.question: Why is using person-first language (e.g., 'a person with schizophrenia' rather than 'a schizophrenic') important when discussing psychological disorders?

options.text: It emphasizes the individual's humanity rather than defining them by their diagnosis
It is required by federal law in all clinical settings
It indicates that the person has been formally diagnosed by a licensed professional
It signals that the disorder is temporary and will eventually resolve

questions.explanation: Person-first language emphasizes the humanity of the individual rather than reducing them to a diagnostic label. This approach helps reduce stigma and promotes compassionate discourse about mental health.
